HERB ROASTED PORK WITH MUSHROOMS : PORK WITH CARAMELIZED ONIONS (VARIATION)

MAIN RECIPE: HERB ROASTED PORK WITH MUSHROOMS

Ingredients:

1.2kg pork shoulder or leg roast
500g mixed mushrooms (cremini, portobello, oyster), quartered
8 cloves garlic, crushed
4 sprigs fresh rosemary
4 sprigs fresh thyme
3 tbsp olive oil
200ml white wine
300ml chicken or pork stock
2 bay leaves
salt and pepper to taste
2 tbsp Dijon mustard

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 180°C.
2. Mix olive oil, mustard, crushed garlic, rosemary, thyme, salt, and pepper.
3. Rub generously all over the pork roast.
4. Heat a large roasting pan over medium-high heat. Brown the roast on all sides, about 10 minutes total.
5. Remove roast and set aside.
6. In the same pan, sauté mushrooms until golden, 5-7 minutes. Remove and set aside.
7. Deglaze pan with white wine, scraping up browned bits.
8. Add stock, bay leaves, and return roast and mushrooms.
9. Cover with foil and roast for 1.5-2 hours until meat is tender (internal temp 70°C).
10. Let rest 15 minutes before carving. Serve with pan juices.

---

VARIATION: PORK WITH CARAMELIZED ONIONS

Substitute mushrooms with:
6 large onions, sliced into half-moons
2 tbsp brown sugar
1 tbsp balsamic vinegar

Method:
1. Follow main recipe steps 1-5.
2. In the roasting pan, cook sliced onions with brown sugar and salt over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until deeply caramelized (20-25 minutes).
3. Add balsamic vinegar, stir well.
4. Return roast to pan, add stock and bay leaves, proceed with roasting as in main recipe.
5. The onions will collapse into a rich, jammy accompaniment to the pork.

Both versions serve 6-8 people. Pair with roasted potatoes or creamed cauliflower.
